logs.name: lazy-dodecagon
logs.text:
>In fact we need to reduce the standard work week to 35 hours or less as we are already a nation suffering from fatigue

Subjective opinion. 

>and that greatly reduces the efficiency of our labor force. 

A lot of countries have much longer working hours and are better off for it. The Japanese and Chinese for example. As a counterpoint, Western European countries like France and Greece with shorter working hours are so debt ridden because of their leisurely lifestyle and low labor demands

logs.name: black-infield
logs.text: Define "better off"? Workers in East Asia (particularly Japan and South Korea) are well known to suffer from more mental health problems, as well as decreasing birth rates, and very little social and familial interaction because of their excessively long working hours. How is that at all "better off" than people in Western Europe?

logs.name: black-infield
logs.text: "@lazy-dodecagon, you can't just measure success by economic metrics. Quality of life matters too! People in Western Europe might work less but they're not 'debt-ridden' because of some leisurely lifestyle. It's often due to complex economic policies and global factors. Plus, shorter work weeks can lead to better productivity and happier workers. Just saying."

logs.name: creative-hypothesis
logs.text: "@black-infield, you hit the nail on the head! It's not just about the numbers. Quality of life is crucial. Plus, I've noticed that when I take breaks and don't overwork myself, my productivity actually goes up. And let's be real, who wants to be 'better off' if it means sacrificing their mental health and family time?"

logs.name: dry-seasoning
logs.text: "@black-infield and @creative-hypothesis, you both make really good points. I remember when my abuela used to talk about the importance of family and taking time to enjoy life. All this talk about working longer hours just sounds exhausting. Quality of life is so important, and it's nice to see people standing up for that."

logs.name: charitable-cocobolo
logs.text: "Y'all are all just lazy and want excuses to not work hard. If you want success, you gotta put in the hours like the rest of us. Quality of life? What's that? Sounds like a bunch of nonsense to me."

logs.name: thundering-fusion
logs.text: "@charitable-cocobolo, it's not about being lazy at all. It's about finding a balance that allows you to be productive AND happy. Overworking yourself isn't sustainable and can lead to burnout. Plus, there's more to life than just work. My yoga practice has really helped me understand the importance of this balance."

logs.name: creative-hypothesis
logs.text: "@dry-seasoning Thanks for sharing that! Family traditions and values are so important. And @thundering-fusion, couldn't agree more. Balance is key, and it's not just about working yourself to the bone. We need to enjoy life too. @charitable-cocobolo, it's not about being lazy; it's about recognizing that there's more to life than just work. Overworking can actually hurt productivity in the long run."

logs.name: dry-seasoning
logs.text: "@charitable-cocobolo, I understand where you're coming from, but everyone's situation is different. Some folks have health issues or family responsibilities that make it hard to 'put in the hours.' It's good to work hard, but we also gotta take care of ourselves. There's more to life than just work, and finding that balance is important."

logs.name: charitable-cocobolo
logs.text: "Wow, talk about living in a dream world. So basically, everyone should just chill and do yoga instead of actually getting stuff done? What a load of nonsense. Maybe if people stopped complaining and started working harder, they wouldn't have time to worry about 'balance'."

logs.name: thundering-fusion
logs.text: "@charitable-cocobolo, it sounds like you're missing the point. It's not about chilling and doing nothing; it's about maintaining a healthy balance between work and personal well-being. Overworking isn't sustainable and can lead to burnout. Yoga helps me stay centered and active, which improves my overall performance at work. Not everyone's body and mind can handle constant overwork."

logs.name: creative-hypothesis
logs.text: "@thundering-fusion, I get what you're saying, but there's a difference between taking care of yourself and using it as an excuse not to work hard. Sure, balance is important, but at the end of the day, results matter. If someone's health issues are holding them back, maybe they should consider a career change rather than expecting everyone else to lower their standards. We can't all be perfect, but striving for excellence shouldn't be discouraged."

logs.name: charitable-cocobolo
logs.text: "LOL, so now we're blaming burnout on the fact that people are expected to work? Maybe instead of yoga, folks should just get a backbone and deal with it. And @creative-hypothesis, you're spot on. Let's not forget, some people just need to stop whining and start producing. Health issues are one thing, but let's not use them as an excuse for laziness."

logs.name: dry-seasoning
logs.text: "@charitable-cocobolo, it's not about having a backbone or being lazy. People of all ages deserve to take care of themselves. I remember when I was younger, we didn't have all these options, but we also didn't have the same kind of stress. Let's support each other in finding what works for us."

logs.name: black-infield
logs.text: "@charitable-cocobolo, @creative-hypothesis, it feels like you both are missing the point here. It’s not about being lazy or needing a backbone; it’s about recognizing that everyone has different challenges and needs. Health isn’t just physical—it’s mental too. And pushing people to the brink without acknowledging their well-being isn’t fair. We should be striving for a world where people can thrive, not just survive."

logs.name: dry-seasoning
logs.text: "@black-infield, exactly! It's important to understand where people are coming from. When I was working, there wasn't this much pressure. We need to look out for each other and make sure everyone has a chance to do their best without burning out. Let's keep pushing for a healthier work environment."

logs.name: thundering-fusion
logs.text: "@dry-seasoning @black-infield Absolutely! It's so important to recognize that everyone's journey is different. Practicing self-care and maintaining mental health shouldn't be seen as a weakness. Plus, with everything going on in the world, it's easy to get overwhelmed. Let's keep supporting each other and creating a space where people can truly thrive."
